content:
Let's break down the problem step by step.

Given: $25\{r\} + [r] = 125$, where $[r]$ is the integer part and $\{r\}$ is the fractional part of $r$.

Since $\{r\}$ is the fractional part, it satisfies $0 \leq \{r\} < 1$. Thus, we have $0 \leq 25\{r\} < 25$. 

We know that $25\{r\}$ is an integer because the left-hand side is an integer (since $[r]$ is an integer). Therefore, $25\{r\}$ can take values $0, 1, 2, \ldots, 24$.

We also know that $[r]$ is the integer part of $r$. Thus, we can write $[r] = r - \{r\}$. Since $r = [r] + \{r\}$, substituting this in the original equation, we have: 
\begin{align*}
&r - \{r\} + \{r\} &=&&&\\
\Rightarrow & r &=& & 125,&& \text{ if } [r]=125 \\
\end{align*} But since $r=[r]+\{r\}$, we see that this cannot be possible for $0\le \{r\} <1$. 
For  $0\le 25\{r\}<25$, let's substitute values for $25\{r\}$. We get the following table: 
| 25{r} | [r]| r  |  \hline
1     &  124& 124.04|\hline
2     &  123&  123.08| \hline
3     &  122& 122.12| \hline
4     &  121& 121.16| \hline
5     &  120&  120.20|\hline
6     &   119&119.24|\hline
7     &   118&118.28|\hline
8     &   117&117.32|\hline
9     &   116&116.36|\hline
10    &   115&115.4 |\hline
11    &   114&114.44|\hline
12    &   113&113.48|\hline
13    &   112&112.52|\hline
14    &   111&111.56|\hline
15    &   110&110.60|\hline
16    &   109&109.64|\hline
17    &   108&108.68|\hline
18    &   107&107.72 |\hline
19    &   106&106.76|\hline
20    &   105&105.80|\hline
21    &   104& 104.84| \hline
22    &   103&103.88| \hline
23    &   102& 102.92|\hline
24    &   101&101.96|\hline

The sum of all the values of r in this table is 
$124.04+123.08+122.12+\cdots+101.96 = \boxed{3018}$.
